Govern-at-merge gate shipped + adversarially proven. Draft PR #207 (qmd-team-intent-kb, branch feat/epic1-govern-at-merge-gate, commit ca34659, NOT merged). mergeGovern() is a re-derivation-over-union pass reusing every existing primitive: union + id-dedup by content-derived UUIDv5, sort-by-id (the one new discipline = commutativity keystone), re-project each row to untrusted MemoryCandidate, assertDisclosureClean() fail-closed first, then full PolicyPipeline with accreting existingHashes seeded from getAllContentHashes(), promote survivors via canonical path with deterministic mergeClock so timestamps + audit chain re-walk identically across clones. Independent auditor (not reusing curator fixtures, called public mergeGovern entrypoint) confirmed gapClosed=true: (a) AWS-key/SSN/salary content rows AND a metadata-buried AWS key all QUARANTINED at the disclosure choke point, absent from durable curated_memories; (b) cross-clone content duplicate deduped to exactly one survivor by content-id; (c) A-union-B vs B-union-A byte-identical governed state (sha256 4ae3c0ee...) AND identical audit chain (sha256 28f0608a...), both verifyAuditChain breaks=0, byte-identity held even when the same union is partitioned across clones differently; (d) every survivor independently re-passed assertDisclosureClean + PolicyPipeline. Differential mutation testing proved tests non-vacuous (bypassing disclosure gate -> secrets rode in; admitting policy-rejected rows -> dedup probe failed), then source reverted byte-identical (sha256 match vs HEAD). Three smuggle vectors all FAILED (metadata free-text secret, content-id collision, reordering attack). 602 tests green; CI all-green (validate/gitleaks/semgrep). Working tree clean, on feature branch. EPIC 1 children 8da.7 (Ed25519 signed DAG anchor) + 8da.8 (merge-aware verifier owning canonical ordering) now build on this.

The bare hash-chain was defeated by tamper-then-rehash (verifyAuditChain breaks:0) but the signed anchor CAUGHT it (HISTORY_REWRITTEN/DAG_HEAD_MISMATCH); a keyless forger failed (20,000 brute signatures, 0 accepted). Governed freshness-on-push SHIPPED (ICO #154 merged, CLEAN — all gates green incl. the slow Stryker mutation-test). Incremental compile: only pages whose bodySha256 changed since last compiled_at are recompiled (deterministic UUID-v5 identity preserved → dedupe still holds), gated by a per-UTC-day cost ceiling + debounce, under a kernel write-lock so concurrent compiles serialize. Each delta still emits the full spool → INTKB govern → receipts (the freshness answer to AutoWiki's on-push, but with GSB's govern+receipt on every delta — the ratified adopt item #1). Gemini's 2 findings FIXED with the PREFERRED refactor: (1) HIGH — the 6 compile sub-passes called process.exit() which bypassed finally{closeDatabase(db)} (DB-handle leak); replaced with a thrown CompilePassError carrying exitCode, caught by the action handler which sets process.exitCode AFTER cleanup runs (+ same-class leak in the --daily-ceiling/--debounce parse guard); (2) MEDIUM — non-sargable cost-gate SQL → sargable compiled_at >= ? AND compiled_at < ? UTC-day range. Tests: compiler 578, cli 517, kernel 423 all green; escape-scan clean.

Govern precision leak FIXED (INTKB #224 merged, CLEAN). Two deterministic (below-the-spool, no model call) fixes: (1) the heroku-api-key bare-UUID rule was flagging ANY UUID in prose as a live credential → over-REJECT of benign memories; added SecretPattern.requiresContext so the UUID rule fires ONLY with key-context nearby (heroku/api key/token/HEROKU_API_KEY= assignment), enforced uniformly across all 3 scanner passes (per-line, newline-collapsed, base64/hex-decode) via execWithContext — the rule was GATED not dropped, so a real Heroku key is still caught; (2) converged classifyContent's PII vocab UP to the boundary filter's (added date-of-birth/ssn-keyword/background-check) so a DOB-only leak is caught.
